---
title: "Title of the Presentation"
subtitle: "Beamer Slides via R Markdown"
author: "Author Name"
institute: "Department · University"
date: "`r format(Sys.time(), '%B %d, %Y')`"
output:
  beamer_presentation:
    slide_level: 2
    keep_tex: true
    latex_engine: xelatex
    highlight: null
fontsize: 10pt
classoption: "aspectratio=169"
header-includes:
  - |
    ```{=latex}
    \RequirePackage{tikz, natbib, setspace}
    \RequirePackage{xcolor}
    \RequirePackage{amsmath, amssymb, amsfonts, amsthm}
    \RequirePackage{mathtools}            % extends amsmath
    \RequirePackage{bm}                   % bold math symbols
    \RequirePackage{verbatim}
    \RequirePackage{hyperref}
    \RequirePackage{listings}
    \RequirePackage{booktabs}             % professional tables
    \RequirePackage{algorithm2e}          % pseudocode

    % ── TikZ libraries ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    \usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta, calc, positioning, shapes.geometric,
                    fit, backgrounds, decorations.pathreplacing,
                    matrix, chains, shadows}

    % ── Colour palette ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    % Primary / structural colours  (rename freely)
    \definecolor{primary}{HTML}{A02337}       % deep crimson
    \definecolor{primarylight}{HTML}{A63547}   % lighter crimson
    \definecolor{accentgrey}{HTML}{58595B}     % neutral grey

    % Functional colours for boxes & emphasis
    \definecolor{infocolor}{HTML}{1A6E8E}     % teal-blue   → info / remark
    \definecolor{successcolor}{HTML}{2E7D32}  % forest green → example / proof
    \definecolor{warncolor}{HTML}{E65100}     % burnt orange → caution / alert
    \definecolor{mathpurple}{HTML}{6A1B9A}    % deep purple  → definition / theorem
    \definecolor{csblue}{HTML}{1565C0}        % royal blue   → algorithm / code
    \definecolor{skyblue}{HTML}{6BA4B8}       % sky blue     → miscellaneous
    \definecolor{lightgrey}{HTML}{F5F5F5}     % near-white   → code backgrounds
    \definecolor{codepurple}{HTML}{7B1FA2}    % listings string colour

    % ── Beamer colour assignments ─────────────────────────────────────
    \setbeamercolor{title}{fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{subtitle}{fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{author}{fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{date}{fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{institute}{fg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{head}{bg=primarylight}

    \setbeamercolor{alerted text}{fg=primary}
    \setbeamercolor{normal text}{fg=darkgray, bg=white}
    \setbeamercolor{structure}{fg=primary}

    \setbeamercolor{jumbo}{bg=primarylight}
    \setbeamercolor{progress bar}{fg=primary, bg=accentgrey}
    \setbeamercolor{progress bar progress}{use=progress bar, bg=progress bar.fg}

    % Default block  (primary)
    \setbeamercolor{block title}{use=structure, fg=white, bg=primarylight}
    \setbeamercolor{block body}{use=structure, fg=darkgray, bg=primarylight!8}

    % Alert block    (warning orange)
    \setbeamercolor{block title alerted}{fg=white, bg=warncolor}
    \setbeamercolor{block body alerted}{fg=darkgray, bg=warncolor!8}

    % Example block  (success green)
    \setbeamercolor{block title example}{fg=white, bg=successcolor}
    \setbeamercolor{block body example}{fg=darkgray, bg=successcolor!8}

    % ── Hyperlinks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    \hypersetup{
        colorlinks,
        linkcolor   = primary,
        anchorcolor = primary,
        citecolor   = infocolor,
        urlcolor    = primarylight,
    }

    % ── Font settings ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
    \setbeamerfont{title}{series=\bfseries, size=\Large}
    \setbeamerfont{subtitle}{series=\mdseries, size=\normalsize}
    \setbeamerfont{author}{size=\small}
    \setbeamerfont{date}{size=\footnotesize}
    \setbeamerfont{institute}{size=\footnotesize}
    \setbeamerfont{alerted text}{series=\bfseries}
    \setbeamerfont{frametitle}{series=\bfseries}
    \setbeamerfont{framesubtitle}{series=\mdseries}
    \setbeamerfont{footline}{size=\scriptsize}
    \setbeamerfont{block title}{size=\normalsize, series=\bfseries}
    \setbeamerfont{block body}{size=\normalsize}

    \usefonttheme{serif}

    % ── Template chrome ────────────────────────────────────────────────
    \setbeamertemplate{navigation symbols}{}
    \setbeamertemplate{blocks}[rounded]
    \setbeamertemplate{itemize item}{\textbullet}
    \setbeamertemplate{itemize subitem}{\textemdash}
    \setbeamertemplate{itemize subsubitem}{\ensuremath{\circ}}
    \setlength{\parskip}{5pt}

    % ── Listings (code) style ──────────────────────────────────────────
    \lstdefinestyle{mystyle}{
        commentstyle   = \color{successcolor!70},
        keywordstyle   = \color{csblue},
        numberstyle    = \tiny\color{accentgrey},
        stringstyle    = \color{codepurple},
        basicstyle     = \ttfamily\scriptsize,
        breakatwhitespace = false,
        breaklines     = true,
        captionpos     = b,
        keepspaces     = true,
        numbers        = left,
        numbersep      = 5pt,
        showspaces     = false,
        showstringspaces = false,
        showtabs       = false,
        tabsize        = 4,
        xleftmargin    = 10pt,
        xrightmargin   = 10pt,
        backgroundcolor = \color{lightgrey},
        frame          = single,
        rulecolor      = \color{accentgrey!30},
    }
    \lstset{style=mystyle}

    % ── Progress-bar footline ──────────────────────────────────────────
    % Use e-TeX dimexpr so Beamer receives a true dimension for the bar width.
    \newdimen\progressbarwidth
    \defbeamertemplate{footline}{progress bar}{%
        \ifnum\value{framenumber}=1\relax
        \else
            \progressbarwidth=\dimexpr\paperwidth*\value{framenumber}/\inserttotalframenumber\relax
            \leavevmode%
            \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=\paperwidth,ht=2pt,dp=1pt]{progress bar}%
                \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=\progressbarwidth,ht=2pt,dp=1pt]{progress bar progress}%
                \end{beamercolorbox}%
            \end{beamercolorbox}%
        \fi
    }
    \setbeamertemplate{footline}[progress bar]

    % ── Back-matter (blank closing slide) ──────────────────────────────
    \newcommand{\backmatter}{
        \begingroup
        \setbeamertemplate{headline}{}
        \setbeamertemplate{footline}{}
        \begin{frame}[c]{}
        \end{frame}
        \endgroup
    }

    % ── Table of contents styling ──────────────────────────────────────
    \setbeamertemplate{section in toc}{
      $\blacktriangleright$~\textbf{\inserttocsection}
    }
    \setbeamertemplate{subsection in toc}{}
    \setbeamertemplate{frametitle continuation}{}

    \AtBeginSection[]{
        \begingroup
        \begin{frame}{Outline}{}
            \tableofcontents[currentsection]
        \end{frame}
        \endgroup
    }

    % ── Headline & frame title ─────────────────────────────────────────
    \makeatletter
    \setbeamertemplate{headline}{
      \ifnum\theframenumber=1
      \else
        \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=\paperwidth,ht=40pt]{head}
        \end{beamercolorbox}
      \fi
    }
    \setbeamertemplate{frametitle}{
        \begin{picture}(0,0)
            \ifx\insertframesubtitle\@empty
                \put(0,27){\usebeamerfont{frametitle}\usebeamercolor[fg]{title}\insertframetitle}
        \else
          \put(0,32){\usebeamerfont{frametitle}\usebeamercolor[fg]{title} \insertframetitle}
          \put(0,20){\usebeamerfont{framesubtitle}\usebeamercolor[fg]{title} \insertframesubtitle }
        \fi
        \end{picture}
    }

    \makeatother

    % ── Title page ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    \setbeamertemplate{title page}{
      \begin{picture}(0,0)
            \put(-5,-77){
                \begin{beamercolorbox}[wd=1.1\paperwidth,ht=140pt]{jumbo}
                \end{beamercolorbox}
            }
        \put(0,25){
          \begin{minipage}{300pt}
            \usebeamerfont{title}\usebeamercolor[fg]{title}\inserttitle\par
            \vspace{10pt}
            \usebeamerfont{subtitle}\usebeamercolor[fg]{subtitle}\insertsubtitle
          \end{minipage}
        }
        \put(0,-45){
          \usebeamerfont{author}\usebeamercolor[fg]{author}\insertauthor
        }
            \put(0,-58){
          \usebeamerfont{institute}\usebeamercolor[fg]{institute}\insertinstitute
        }
        \put(0,-70){
          \usebeamerfont{date}\usebeamercolor[fg]{date}\insertdate
        }
      \end{picture}
    }

    % ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    %  CUSTOM COLOURED BOXES  (for emphasis during presentations)
    % ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

    % ── Info / Remark box (teal-blue) ──────────────────────────────────
    \newenvironment{infobox}[1][Remark]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=infocolor}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=infocolor!8}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ── Caution / Warning box (orange) ─────────────────────────────────
    \newenvironment{cautionbox}[1][Caution]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=warncolor}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=warncolor!8}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ── Definition box (purple) ────────────────────────────────────────
    \newenvironment{defbox}[1][Definition]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=mathpurple}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=mathpurple!8}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ── Theorem box (deep blue) ────────────────────────────────────────
    \newenvironment{thmbox}[1][Theorem]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=csblue}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=csblue!8}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ── Proof sketch box (green, no title bar) ─────────────────────────
    \newenvironment{proofbox}[1][Proof]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=successcolor!80}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=successcolor!6}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ── Algorithm / Code box (royal blue) ──────────────────────────────
    \newenvironment{algobox}[1][Algorithm]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=csblue!90}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=csblue!6}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ── Highlight / Key-takeaway box (primary crimson) ─────────────────
    \newenvironment{keybox}[1][Key Takeaway]{%
      \setbeamercolor{block title}{fg=white, bg=primary}%
      \setbeamercolor{block body}{fg=darkgray, bg=primary!8}%
      \begin{block}{#1}}{\end{block}}

    % ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    %  MATH SHORTCUTS  (optional — delete any you don't need)
    % ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
    \newcommand{\E}{\mathbb{E}}
    \newcommand{\Var}{\operatorname{Var}}
    \newcommand{\Cov}{\operatorname{Cov}}
    \newcommand{\Prob}{\mathbb{P}}
    \newcommand{\R}{\mathbb{R}}
    \newcommand{\N}{\mathbb{N}}
    \newcommand{\Z}{\mathbb{Z}}
    \newcommand{\indep}{\perp\!\!\!\perp}
    \DeclareMathOperator*{\argmin}{arg\,min}
    \DeclareMathOperator*{\argmax}{arg\,max}
    \DeclareMathOperator{\plim}{plim}
    \newcommand{\iid}{\overset{\text{iid}}{\sim}}
    \newcommand{\convp}{\xrightarrow{p}}
    \newcommand{\convd}{\xrightarrow{d}}
    \newcommand{\aseq}{\overset{\text{a.s.}}{=}}
    \newcommand{\norm}[1]{\left\lVert #1 \right\rVert}
    \newcommand{\abs}[1]{\left\lvert #1 \right\rvert}
    ```
---


```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# Global chunk options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo        = FALSE,
  message     = FALSE,
  warning     = FALSE,
  fig.width   = 6,
  fig.height  = 3,
  out.width   = "70%",
  fig.align   = "center",
  size        = "footnotesize"
)

# Packages (add / remove as needed)
# library(tidyverse)
# library(modelsummary)
# library(fixest)
# library(kableExtra)

# Custom ggplot theme
# Colour-hex values match the beamer palette for visual consistency
primary_col     <- "#A02337"
infocolor_col   <- "#1A6E8E"
success_col     <- "#2E7D32"
csblue_col      <- "#1565C0"
accentgrey_col  <- "#58595B"

palette_main <- c(primary_col, csblue_col, infocolor_col,
                  success_col, "#E65100", "#6A1B9A")

theme_slides <- function(base_size = 11) {
  theme_minimal(base_size = base_size) %+replace%
    theme(
      legend.position  = "bottom",
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      plot.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = rel(1.1)),
      axis.title       = element_text(size = rel(0.9))
    )
}

# modelsummary formatting (for regression tables)
gof <- tibble::tribble(
  ~raw,           ~clean,          ~fmt,
  "nobs",         "N",             0,
  "r.squared",    "$R^2$",         2,
  "adj.r.squared","Adj.~$R^2$",    2,
  "rmse",         "RMSE",          3
)

# Dynamic font-size hook
def.chunk.hook <- knitr::knit_hooks$get("chunk")
knitr::knit_hooks$set(chunk = function(x, options) {
  x <- def.chunk.hook(x, options)
  ifelse(options$size != "normalsize",
         paste0("\n \\", options$size, "\n\n", x, "\n\n \\normalsize"),
         x)
})
```


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 1 — COLOUR PALETTE & BOX SHOWCASE
     ================================================================ -->

# Template Showcase

## Built-in Beamer Blocks

Beamer ships with three block types.

::: {.block}
### Default Block
Use for neutral information, references, or summaries.
:::

::: {.alertblock}
### Alert Block
Draw attention to caveats, assumptions, or pitfalls.
:::

::: {.exampleblock}
### Example Block
Worked examples, data excerpts, or code output.
:::


## Custom Emphasis Boxes

\begin{infobox}[Remark]
  Use \texttt{infobox} for supplementary notes, data-source caveats,
  or interpretation guidance.
\end{infobox}

\begin{cautionbox}[Caution]
  Use \texttt{cautionbox} when an assumption is critical:
  e.g.\ ``strict exogeneity may fail under simultaneity.''
\end{cautionbox}

\begin{defbox}[Definition — Conditional Expectation]
  $\E[Y \mid X] = \int y \, f_{Y|X}(y \mid x) \, dy$
\end{defbox}


## Custom Emphasis Boxes (cont.)

\begin{thmbox}[Gauss--Markov Theorem]
  Under assumptions A1--A5, the OLS estimator $\hat{\beta}$
  is \textbf{BLUE}: Best Linear Unbiased Estimator.
\end{thmbox}

\begin{proofbox}[Proof Sketch]
  Show $\Var(\tilde\beta) - \Var(\hat\beta)$ is positive
  semi-definite for any alternative linear unbiased $\tilde\beta$.
\end{proofbox}

\begin{keybox}[Key Takeaway]
  Unbiasedness alone is not enough — we also need minimum variance
  among the class of linear unbiased estimators.
\end{keybox}


## Colour Reference Card

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    swatch/.style={
      minimum width=1.3cm, minimum height=0.65cm,
      rounded corners=2pt, font=\scriptsize\sffamily, text=white
    }
  ]
  \node[swatch, fill=primary]      at (0,0)   {primary};
  \node[swatch, fill=primarylight] at (1.8,0) {primarylight};
  \node[swatch, fill=accentgrey]   at (3.6,0) {accentgrey};
  \node[swatch, fill=infocolor]    at (5.4,0) {infocolor};
  \node[swatch, fill=successcolor] at (7.2,0) {successcolor};
  \node[swatch, fill=warncolor]    at (9.0,0) {warncolor};
  \node[swatch, fill=mathpurple]   at (10.8,0){mathpurple};
  \node[swatch, fill=csblue]       at (12.6,0){csblue};
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}

All colours are defined once in the YAML header — change a single
`\definecolor` line and the entire deck updates.


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 2 — MATHEMATICS
     ================================================================ -->

# Mathematics

## Optimisation Problem

\begin{defbox}[Empirical Risk Minimisation]
\vspace{-4pt}
\[
  \hat\theta = \argmin_{\theta \in \Theta}\;
  \frac{1}{n} \sum_{i=1}^{n} \ell\!\bigl(y_i,\; f(x_i;\theta)\bigr)
  + \lambda\, \Omega(\theta)
\]
where $\ell$ is a loss function and $\Omega(\theta)$ is a regulariser
(e.g.\ $\norm{\theta}_2^2$ for ridge, $\norm{\theta}_1$ for lasso).
\end{defbox}

\begin{infobox}[Remark]
  When $\ell$ is the negative log-likelihood and $\lambda=0$,
  ERM reduces to maximum likelihood estimation (MLE).
\end{infobox}


## Probability \& Convergence

\begin{thmbox}[Central Limit Theorem]
  Let $X_1, X_2, \ldots \iid (\mu, \sigma^2)$ with
  $\sigma^2 < \infty$.  Then
  \[
    \sqrt{n}\,(\bar X_n - \mu) \;\convd\; \mathcal{N}(0,\,\sigma^2).
  \]
\end{thmbox}

\begin{proofbox}[Proof Idea]
  Use the characteristic-function approach:
  $\varphi_{\bar X_n}(t) \to e^{-\sigma^2 t^2/2}$
  by Taylor expansion of $\log\varphi_X$.
\end{proofbox}


## Aligned Equations

Multi-line derivations render cleanly with `align*`:

\begin{align*}
  \hat\beta_{\text{OLS}}
    &= (X'X)^{-1} X'y \\
    &= \beta + (X'X)^{-1} X'\varepsilon \\
  \E[\hat\beta \mid X]
    &= \beta + (X'X)^{-1} X' \underbrace{\E[\varepsilon \mid X]}_{=\,0}
     = \beta.
\end{align*}

\begin{infobox}[Remark]
  Unbiasedness requires $\E[\varepsilon \mid X] = 0$
  (strict exogeneity), which is stronger than
  $\E[\varepsilon_i x_i] = 0$ (mean independence).
\end{infobox}


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 3 — ECONOMETRICS
     ================================================================ -->

# Econometrics

## Instrumental Variables

\begin{defbox}[IV Estimator]
Given instrument matrix $Z$ satisfying \textbf{relevance}
($\text{rank}\, \E[Z'X] = k$) and \textbf{exogeneity}
($\E[Z'\varepsilon] = 0$):
\[
  \hat\beta_{\text{IV}} = (Z'X)^{-1} Z'y.
\]
The two-stage least-squares (2SLS) generalisation for
over-identification is
$\hat\beta_{2\text{SLS}} = (X'P_Z X)^{-1} X'P_Z y$,
where $P_Z = Z(Z'Z)^{-1}Z'$.
\end{defbox}

\begin{cautionbox}[Weak Instruments]
  If the first-stage $F$-statistic is below $\approx 10$,
  2SLS is severely biased toward OLS; consider LIML or
  Anderson--Rubin confidence sets.
\end{cautionbox}


## Regression Table (modelsummary)

```{r reg-table, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, results='asis'}
# Uncomment when fixest / modelsummary are available
# library(fixest) ; library(modelsummary)
# m1 <- feols(y ~ x1,              data = df)
# m2 <- feols(y ~ x1 + x2,         data = df)
# m3 <- feols(y ~ x1 + x2 | fe_id, data = df)
# modelsummary(
#   list("(1)" = m1, "(2)" = m2, "(3)" = m3),
#   output   = "latex_tabular",
#   stars    = c("*" = .1, "**" = .05, "***" = .01),
#   gof_map  = gof,
#   coef_rename = c("x1" = "Treatment", "x2" = "Control Var")
# )
```

\begin{center}\scriptsize
\begin{tabular}{lccc}
\toprule
 & (1) & (2) & (3) \\
\midrule
Treatment       & 0.52$^{***}$ & 0.48$^{***}$ & 0.45$^{**}$  \\
                & (0.12)       & (0.11)       & (0.18)       \\
Control Var     &              & 0.33$^{**}$  & 0.30$^{*}$   \\
                &              & (0.15)       & (0.16)       \\
\midrule
Fixed Effects   & No  & No  & Yes \\
$N$             & 500 & 500 & 500 \\
$R^2$           & 0.08 & 0.12 & 0.35 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 4 — COMPUTER SCIENCE & ALGORITHMS
     ================================================================ -->

# Computer Science

## Pseudocode with \texttt{algorithm2e}

\begin{algobox}[Gradient Descent]
\vspace{-2pt}
\begin{algorithm}[H]
\DontPrintSemicolon
\SetAlgoNoLine
\KwIn{Learning rate $\eta$, initial $\theta^{(0)}$, tolerance $\epsilon$}
\KwOut{$\hat\theta$}
$t \gets 0$\;
\Repeat{$\norm{\nabla L(\theta^{(t)})} < \epsilon$}{
  $g \gets \nabla_\theta L(\theta^{(t)})$\;
  $\theta^{(t+1)} \gets \theta^{(t)} - \eta\, g$\;
  $t \gets t + 1$\;
}
\Return{$\theta^{(t)}$}\;
\end{algorithm}
\vspace{-4pt}
\end{algobox}


## Code Listing (Python) {.fragile}

\begin{lstlisting}[language=Python, caption={Ridge regression via closed form}]
import numpy as np

def ridge(X, y, lam=1.0):
    """Closed-form ridge regression."""
    n, p = X.shape
    I = np.eye(p)
    beta = np.linalg.solve(X.T @ X + lam * I, X.T @ y)
    return beta
\end{lstlisting}


## Code Listing (R) {.fragile}

\begin{lstlisting}[language=R, caption={Fixed-effects panel regression}]
library(fixest)

# Estimate with two-way fixed effects
m <- feols(
  log(wage) ~ experience + education | firm + year,
  data   = panel_df,
  cluster = ~firm
)
summary(m)
\end{lstlisting}


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 5 — TikZ DIAGRAMS
     ================================================================ -->

# Diagrams with TikZ

## Machine-Learning Pipeline

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    node distance=0.6cm and 0.8cm,
    stage/.style={
      rectangle, rounded corners=4pt,
      minimum width=2.2cm, minimum height=0.9cm,
      font=\small\sffamily, text=white, align=center,
      drop shadow={shadow xshift=0.5pt, shadow yshift=-0.5pt,
                   fill=black!25}
    },
    arr/.style={-{Stealth[length=5pt]}, thick, accentgrey},
  ]
  % nodes
  \node[stage, fill=accentgrey]   (raw)   {Raw Data};
  \node[stage, fill=infocolor, right=of raw]   (clean) {Clean \&\\Wrangle};
  \node[stage, fill=csblue,    right=of clean] (feat)  {Feature\\Engineering};
  \node[stage, fill=mathpurple,right=of feat]  (model) {Model\\Training};
  \node[stage, fill=successcolor,right=of model](eval)  {Evaluate};
  \node[stage, fill=primary,   right=of eval]  (deploy){Deploy};
  % arrows
  \draw[arr] (raw)   -- (clean);
  \draw[arr] (clean) -- (feat);
  \draw[arr] (feat)  -- (model);
  \draw[arr] (model) -- (eval);
  \draw[arr] (eval)  -- (deploy);
  % feedback loop
  \draw[arr, warncolor, dashed]
    (eval.south) -- ++(0,-0.55) -| node[below, pos=0.25,
    font=\scriptsize\sffamily, text=warncolor]{retrain} (model.south);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}


## Econometric Identification Strategy (DAG)

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    node distance=1.5cm and 2.4cm,
    var/.style={
      circle, draw, thick, minimum size=1cm,
      font=\small\sffamily, inner sep=2pt
    },
    arr/.style={-{Stealth[length=5pt]}, thick},
  ]
  % nodes
  \node[var]                      (X)  {$X$};
  \node[var, right=of X]          (Y)  {$Y$};
  \node[var, above right=0.9cm and 1.2cm of X,
        fill=warncolor!15, draw=warncolor] (U)  {$U$};
  \node[var, left=of X,
        fill=csblue!15, draw=csblue]       (Z)  {$Z$};

  % edges
  \draw[arr]                (X) -- node[below, font=\scriptsize]{causal?} (Y);
  \draw[arr, warncolor]     (U) -- (X);
  \draw[arr, warncolor]     (U) -- (Y);
  \draw[arr, csblue]        (Z) -- node[below, font=\scriptsize]{1st stage} (X);

  % annotations
  \node[font=\scriptsize\sffamily, text=warncolor, above=0.05cm of U]
    {Unobserved confounder};
  \node[font=\scriptsize\sffamily, text=csblue, below=0.05cm of Z]
    {Instrument};
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}

\begin{infobox}[Reading the DAG]
  $Z$ affects $Y$ \emph{only} through $X$ (exclusion restriction).
  The confounder $U$ is the reason OLS is inconsistent.
\end{infobox}


## Neural-Network Architecture

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    node distance=1.2cm and 1.6cm,
    neuron/.style={circle, draw, thick, minimum size=0.55cm,
                   inner sep=0pt, font=\tiny},
    arr/.style={-{Stealth[length=3pt]}, thin, accentgrey!70},
    layer/.style={font=\scriptsize\sffamily, text=accentgrey},
  ]
  % Input layer
  \foreach \i in {1,2,3,4}{
    \node[neuron, fill=csblue!20, draw=csblue]
      (I\i) at (0, -\i*0.7+1.75) {};
  }
  \node[layer, above=0.15cm of I1] {Input};

  % Hidden layer 1
  \foreach \i in {1,2,3,4,5}{
    \node[neuron, fill=mathpurple!20, draw=mathpurple]
      (H1\i) at (2, -\i*0.7+2.1) {};
  }
  \node[layer, above=0.15cm of H11] {Hidden 1};

  % Hidden layer 2
  \foreach \i in {1,2,3,4,5}{
    \node[neuron, fill=mathpurple!20, draw=mathpurple]
      (H2\i) at (4, -\i*0.7+2.1) {};
  }
  \node[layer, above=0.15cm of H21] {Hidden 2};

  % Output layer
  \foreach \i in {1,2}{
    \node[neuron, fill=primary!20, draw=primary]
      (O\i) at (6, -\i*0.7+0.7) {};
  }
  \node[layer, above=0.15cm of O1] {Output};

  % Connections
  \foreach \i in {1,...,4}
    \foreach \j in {1,...,5}
      \draw[arr] (I\i) -- (H1\j);
  \foreach \i in {1,...,5}
    \foreach \j in {1,...,5}
      \draw[arr] (H1\i) -- (H2\j);
  \foreach \i in {1,...,5}
    \foreach \j in {1,...,2}
      \draw[arr] (H2\i) -- (O\j);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}


## Software Architecture (Layered)

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    box/.style={
      rectangle, rounded corners=3pt,
      minimum width=10cm, minimum height=0.85cm,
      font=\small\sffamily, text=white, align=center
    },
  ]
  \node[box, fill=csblue]       at (0, 0)    {Presentation Layer \quad (API / UI)};
  \node[box, fill=infocolor]    at (0,-1.15) {Application Layer \quad (Business Logic)};
  \node[box, fill=mathpurple]   at (0,-2.3)  {Domain Layer \quad (Models / Entities)};
  \node[box, fill=accentgrey]   at (0,-3.45) {Infrastructure Layer \quad (DB / Cache / MQ)};
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}


## Decision Flowchart

\begin{center}
\begin{tikzpicture}[
    node distance=0.6cm and 1.3cm,
    process/.style={rectangle, rounded corners=3pt, draw=csblue,
                    fill=csblue!8, minimum width=2.5cm,
                    minimum height=0.7cm, font=\small\sffamily,
                    align=center},
    decision/.style={diamond, draw=warncolor, fill=warncolor!8,
                     minimum width=1.6cm, minimum height=0.8cm,
                     font=\small\sffamily, align=center,
                     aspect=2.2, inner sep=1pt},
    arr/.style={-{Stealth[length=5pt]}, thick, accentgrey},
  ]
  \node[process]                     (start)  {Collect Data};
  \node[decision, below=of start]    (eda)    {$n > p$\,?};
  \node[process,  left=of eda]       (reg)    {Regularised\\Regression};
  \node[process,  right=of eda]      (ols)    {OLS / MLE};
  \node[decision, below=of eda]      (cv)     {CV error\\acceptable?};
  \node[process,  below=of cv]       (report) {Report Results};
  \node[process,  right=of cv]       (tune)   {Tune $\lambda$ /\\Add Features};

  \draw[arr] (start) -- (eda);
  \draw[arr] (eda)   -- node[above,font=\scriptsize]{No}  (reg);
  \draw[arr] (eda)   -- node[above,font=\scriptsize]{Yes} (ols);
  \draw[arr] (reg)   |- (cv);
  \draw[arr] (ols)   |- (cv);
  \draw[arr] (cv)    -- node[left,font=\scriptsize]{Yes} (report);
  \draw[arr] (cv)    -- node[above,font=\scriptsize]{No}  (tune);
  \draw[arr, dashed] (tune) |- (eda);
\end{tikzpicture}
\end{center}


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 6 — DATA SCIENCE / R PLOTS
     ================================================================ -->

# Data Visualisation

## Slide with R Output {.fragile}

```{r cars-summary, echo=TRUE}
summary(cars)
```

## Slide with Plot

```{r pressure-plot, fig.cap="Vapor pressure of mercury."}
plot(pressure, pch = 19, col = "#A02337")
```


## ggplot2 Example {.fragile}

```{r ggplot-demo, echo=TRUE, eval=TRUE, fig.height=2.6}
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(mpg, aes(displ, hwy, colour = factor(cyl))) +
  geom_point(alpha = .7, size = 2) +
  labs(x = "Displacement (L)", y = "Highway MPG",
       colour = "Cylinders") +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("#1565C0","#2E7D32",
                                 "#E65100","#A02337")) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```


<!-- ================================================================
     SECTION 7 — TABLES WITH kableExtra
     ================================================================ -->

# Tables

## Formatted Table (kableExtra)

```{r kable-demo, eval=TRUE}
library(kableExtra)
head(mtcars[, 1:6], 6) |>
  kbl(booktabs = TRUE, caption = "Motor Trend Cars (excerpt)",
      linesep = "") |>
  kable_styling(font_size = 8,
                latex_options = c("hold_position")) |>
  row_spec(0, bold = TRUE, color = "white", background = "#A63547")
```


<!-- ================================================================
     CLOSING
     ================================================================ -->

# Summary

## Recap

\begin{keybox}[What This Template Provides]
\begin{itemize}
  \item \textbf{8 custom colour boxes} — definition, theorem, proof,
        info, caution, algorithm, key takeaway, plus 3 built-in Beamer blocks.
  \item \textbf{TikZ diagrams} — pipelines, DAGs, neural nets,
        flowcharts, layered architectures; all using palette colours.
  \item \textbf{Math shortcuts} — $\E$, $\Var$, $\Cov$, $\Prob$,
        $\argmin$, $\plim$, $\convp$, $\convd$, $\norm{\cdot}$, etc.
  \item \textbf{Listings \& algorithm2e} — syntax-highlighted code +
        pseudocode out of the box.
  \item \textbf{Consistent palette} — change one
        \texttt{\textbackslash definecolor} line and the whole deck follows.
\end{itemize}
\end{keybox}


## Thank You {.plain}

\begin{center}
  {\Large\bfseries Questions?}

  \vspace{10pt}
  \textcolor{accentgrey}{
    Author Name \quad $\cdot$ \quad \texttt{author@university.edu}
  }
\end{center}
